en.extract:
The Sokoke pipit (Anthus sokokensis) is a species of bird in the family Motacillidae.
It is found in Kenya and Tanzania.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
It is threatened by habitat loss. It has richly coloured upperparts, prominent pale wingbars, and a heavily streaked breast.


References


External links

BirdLife Species Factsheet.

The '''Sokoke pipit''' ('''''Anthus sokokensis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Motacillidae]].
It is found in [[Kenya]] and [[Tanzania]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.
It is threatened by [[habitat loss]]. It has richly coloured upperparts, prominent pale wingbars, and a heavily streaked breast.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hbw.com/species/sokoke-pipit-anthus-sokokensis|title=Sokoke Pipit (Anthus sokokensis)|website=www.hbw.com|language=en|access-date=2018-07-31}}</ref>
